title: compliance — trait composition
description: Four owned traits mixed into downstream models. `HasConsent` gives a model consent-tracking helpers keyed on its own identity. `IsRetentionAware` is a marker so the retention runner picks the model up. `HasLegalHold` gives freeze-check helpers. `IsDsarContributor` is a marker composed on models participating in DSAR collection. None wraps a vendor package — compliance is a first-party module.

owned.description: Mixed into any Eloquent model that represents a consent subject (User, Guardian, TenantContact, future subjects). Adds runtime helpers: `->consents()` (all ConsentRecord rows for this subject), `->consentFor(string $category)` (latest decision), `->grantConsent(string $category, array $meta = [])`, `->withdrawConsent(string $category)`, `->hasActiveConsent(string $category): bool` (delegates to ConsentGate; cached). Boot hook wires the subject_id column to the trait-composing model's primary key.
owned.note: Trait adds NO columns to the composing model — subject identity is the model's own primary key. The relationship lands on ConsentRecord.subject_type + ConsentRecord.subject_id.

owned.description: Marker + minimal helper trait for models participating in the retention runner. The RetentionRunner uses `retention.json` for the model's canonical window; this trait's runtime is a single accessor `->retentionEffective(): RetentionOutcome` returning the resolved window after LegalHold + tenant override checks. Composed by every model that lives past its expected purge (Files, ActivityLog entries, TransferArtifacts, etc.) so the runner has one API surface.

Mixed into models that can be frozen from purges. Adds `->isLegallyHeld(): bool` and a global query scope `withoutLegallyHeld()`. The trait registers the model against `LegalHoldGate` at boot so the gate can enumerate held rows across every model class from one seam. Composed alongside `IsRetentionAware` on models whose purges must respect litigation freezes.
owned.note: The hold data lives entirely in the compliance::LegalHold table — the trait never adds a `hold_id` column to the composing model. `isLegallyHeld()` is a query against LegalHold with the composing model's identity + tenant_id.

owned.description: Marker trait composed on models that contribute to DSAR bundles. Complements the `#[DsarExportable]` / `#[DsarErasable]` class attributes — the attributes carry the CONFIGURATION (which column is the subject, what to include / exclude, erasure strategy); the trait provides the RUNTIME hooks (`->dsarExportRow(SubjectId $s): array`, `->dsarErase(SubjectId $s): void`). Downstream modules override the trait hooks when their contribution needs custom shape (e.g. streaming rows for large exports).

framework.reason: Dsar, LegalHold, Subprocessor, SafeguardingIncident. NOT ConsentRecord (immutable) or RetentionRun (audit-only, hard-purge).

tenants.reason: Every entity except platform-default ConsentCategory rows + platform-default Subprocessor rows carries tenant_id.

activity.consumed_by: Dsar, SafeguardingIncident, LegalHold. NOT ConsentRecord (audit target already via HasAuditable; adding activity would double-log every consent change into the tenant feed — too noisy).

consent_category.notes: NO BelongsToTenant — platform-default rows carry tenant_id=NULL; tenant overrides carry a tenant_id. Global scope resolution merges the two sets. NO SoftDeletes — categories are HardDelete only (deleting a category with active ConsentRecords refused by observer). NO HasActivityLog.

consent_record.notes: NO SoftDeletes — consent records are IMMUTABLE. NO HasActivityLog (see consumed.activity above). Withdrawal writes a new row rather than mutating; the ConsentRecordObserver refuses UPDATE on all fields except `metadata` (append-only).

dsar.notes: SoftDeletes so a cancelled DSAR can be inspected in retro before hard-purge. HasActivityLog puts the tenant-facing lifecycle transitions in the activity feed for the requester's own visibility.

dsar_artefact.notes: SoftDeletes cascades from parent Dsar. NO HasAuditable (parent Dsar carries the compliance audit trail; artefact deltas would double-log). NO HasActivityLog.

legal_hold.notes: Full audit-material trait stack. SoftDeletes so released holds can be inspected during litigation retros. HasActivityLog for platform-admin visibility.

retention_run.notes: High-volume table (one row per tenant per sweep). NO SoftDeletes / HasUserstamps / HasActivityLog / HasAuditable. Hard-purged after 365 days (see retention.json).

subprocessor.notes: NO BelongsToTenant — subprocessors are a PLATFORM-LEVEL registry (tenant_id=NULL always). Full audit trail because every change is a DPA amendment. Versioning handled by an internal `version` integer + a SubprocessorVersion snapshot table (not exposed as a separate module entity; managed within Subprocessor observers).

safeguarding_incident.notes: Every touch is audit-material. HasActivityLog puts state transitions in the tenant safeguarding officer's feed. SoftDeletes lets a closed_no_action row be re-opened when new information surfaces.
